title: delphinium candelabrum var. monanthum
intro: Delphinium candelabrum var. monanthum, also known as Monk's Delight or Monk's Rampion, is a rare and unusual variety of the Delphinium candelabrum plant, a type of flowering perennial in the buttercup family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, specifically in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Delphinium candelabrum var. monanthum include its ability to help reduce coughing and relieve congestion, making it a useful natural remedy for people suffering from respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, which can be made by steeping the roots or leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
